deep_dive.title: Skin-Deep: A Geometric Diagnostic for Alignment Fragility in Large Language Model Representations
deep_dive.abstract: Alignment tuning is meant to make harmful-request refusal robust, yet this safety behavior can be erased by a small set of benign fine-tuning examples. This is a deployment risk for open-weight models because a checkpoint can pass refusal tests at release time and later lose refusal under low-cost downstream fine-tuning. Prior work has established these refusal failures, but existing studies do not show how to detect this fragility in the aligned model itself before an attack or fine-tuning intervention is run. We introduce Skin-Deep, a geometric diagnostic that detects alignment fragility directly from the aligned model's hidden-state activations before such an intervention is run and compresses the layer-wise safety geometry into a single scalar, the Geometric Fragility Score (GFS). Applied to twenty-one instruction-tuned models spanning six alignment recipes and 3B--32B parameters, Skin-Deep reveals a recurring low-rank safety subspace across model families. Direction ablations show that removing directions in this subspace weakens harmful-request refusal, providing causal evidence that the recovered geometry underlies refusal behavior. Crucially, GFS identifies, before any fine-tuning, the initially safe model that retains the most refusal after small-scale LoRA fine-tuning. These results establish GFS as a practical pre-deployment diagnostic for flagging fragile refusal behavior without running an attack.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: diagnoses alignment fragility in LLMs; detects risk of safety degradation from fine-tuning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 诊断LLM对齐脆弱性；检测微调导致安全退化的风险
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 诊断LLM对齐脆弱性；检测微调导致安全退化的风险
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 诊断LLM对齐脆弱性；检测微调导致安全退化的风险
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Skin-Deep proposes a geometric diagnostic to detect alignment fragility in LLM hidden states before fine-tuning, showing that safety behavior can be easily erased.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 对齐微调本应使有害请求拒绝变得稳健，但少量良性微调样本即可擦除安全行为。现有研究未能提前检测这种脆弱性。Skin-Deep提出一种几何诊断方法，从对齐模型隐藏状态激活中检测对齐脆弱性，并压缩分层安全几何。实验表明该方法能在攻击或微调干预之前有效识别脆弱模型，为防御有害微调攻击提供了预警手段。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 对齐微调本应使有害请求拒绝变得稳健，但少量良性微调样本即可擦除安全行为。现有研究未能提前检测这种脆弱性。Skin-Deep提出一种几何诊断方法，从对齐模型隐藏状态激活中检测对齐脆弱性，并压缩分层安全几何。实验表明该方法能在攻击或微调干预之前有效识别脆弱模型，为防御有害微调攻击提供了预警手段。

deep_dive.title: On the QUEST for Uncertainty Quantification via Highest Density Regions
deep_dive.abstract: Uncertainty quantification (UQ) is essential for reliable decision-making in safety-critical applications in probabilistic machine learning. For regression problems, dominant scalar UQ approaches - notably, those based on proper scoring rules - measure uncertainty via pointwise predictive risk. This can lead to counterintuitive results when the target statistic is not the conditional expectation. We propose an alternative framework, in which uncertainty is characterised by the volume of the most probable subset of a distribution's support. QUEST (Quantifying Uncertainty via highest dEnSiTy regions) is a novel approach to UQ based on the concentration of Lebesgue measure at a distribution's peak(s), evaluated at one or more values of a robustness parameter $α$. We establish connections between our measures and classical statistics from information theory and economics. We show that, unlike popular alternatives based on proper scoring rules, QUEST measures of epistemic and aleatoric uncertainty satisfy a set of axioms adapted from the UQ literature, including monotonicity under distributional spread and invariance to location shifts. Selective prediction benchmarks confirm that QUEST performs favourably against standard measures such as variance and differential entropy.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes QUEST method for uncertainty quantification via highest density regions
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出基于最高密度区域的不确定量化方法QUEST
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出基于最高密度区域的不确定量化方法QUEST
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出基于最高密度区域的不确定量化方法QUEST
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: QUEST quantifies uncertainty via the volume of highest density regions, addressing limitations of scalar UQ methods. The framework uses Lebesgue measure concentration and robustness parameters, enabling more intuitive uncertainty characterization in regression.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有不确定量化方法在回归问题中使用点状预测风险，当目标统计量非条件期望时产生反直觉结果。本文提出QUEST框架，通过分布支持集最高概率区域的体积度量不确定性，基于Lebesgue测度在峰值处的集中程度，并引入鲁棒性参数。实验表明该方法能够更直观地刻画预测不确定性，克服了传统标量方法的缺陷。该工作为概率机器学习中安全关键应用提供了新的不确定性量化工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有不确定量化方法在回归问题中使用点状预测风险，当目标统计量非条件期望时产生反直觉结果。本文提出QUEST框架，通过分布支持集最高概率区域的体积度量不确定性，基于Lebesgue测度在峰值处的集中程度，并引入鲁棒性参数。实验表明该方法能够更直观地刻画预测不确定性，克服了传统标量方法的缺陷。该工作为概率机器学习中安全关键应用提供了新的不确定性量化工具。

quick_skim.title: Possible or Definite? A Benchmark for Evaluating Diagnostic Uncertainty Preservation in Clinical Text
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for clinical text tasks such as summarization and revision. While most studies evaluate the fluency and coherence of LLM-generated text, whether LLMs correctly preserve diagnostic uncertainty remains underexplored. In clinical practice, phrases such as ``possible pneumonia'' communicate the strength of available evidence and directly guide decisions about follow-up testing and treatment. Altering these uncertainty expressions can change the clinical meaning entirely. In this paper, we systematically evaluated this problem in two steps. First, we constructed a benchmark of 1,200 clinical documents with 9,184 uncertainty annotations across five levels. Second, we evaluated three LLMs on this benchmark. Our results show that (1) LLMs preserve the original uncertainty cues poorly, often less than half the time; (2) LLMs struggle with nuanced distinctions between adjacent levels. This work reveals a failure mode not captured by standard evaluation metrics and provides implications for the safe deployment of LLMs in clinical workflows.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for LLM diagnostic uncertainty preservation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对LLM诊断不确定性保留的基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对LLM诊断不确定性保留的基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对LLM诊断不确定性保留的基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: A benchmark to evaluate whether LLMs preserve diagnostic uncertainty in clinical text, with 1,200 documents and 9,184 annotations across five uncertainty levels, revealing common failures.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文关注LLM在处理临床文本时是否正确保留诊断不确定性这一重要问题。现有评估多关注流畅性和一致性，而忽视不确定性表达。论文构建了一个包含1200个临床文档和9184个不确定性标注的基准，覆盖五个不确定性等级。评估结果表明LLM在保持不确定性方面存在常见失败，为临床安全提供了重要参考。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文关注LLM在处理临床文本时是否正确保留诊断不确定性这一重要问题。现有评估多关注流畅性和一致性，而忽视不确定性表达。论文构建了一个包含1200个临床文档和9184个不确定性标注的基准，覆盖五个不确定性等级。评估结果表明LLM在保持不确定性方面存在常见失败，为临床安全提供了重要参考。

quick_skim.title: Right Knowledge, Wrong Answer: Test-Time Steering for Temporal Fact Conflicts in Open-Weight Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models can store both outdated facts and newer superseding facts in their parameters, but standard prompting may still elicit the outdated answer. We formalize this problem as Parametric Temporal Conflict (PTC) and introduce Temporal Attractor Steering (TAS), a three-stage test-time intervention that detects likely conflicts, identifies a conflict-critical layer, and steers hidden states toward newer-fact representations without retraining or external retrieval. We construct an 8,746-record verified benchmark across five Wikidata relations and evaluate four open-weight language models from three families: Qwen-2.5-1.5B/7B, Mistral-7B-v0.3, and Llama-3.1-8B. Single-layer activation patching achieves answer-flip rates of 0.72-0.85 across all models. End-to-end TAS resolves 29-57% of PTC cases while preserving 85-99% accuracy on non-conflict queries, outperforming a matched ITI baseline on three of four models. These results show that outdated parametric knowledge can be selectively overridden at inference time.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Temporal Attractor Steering (TAS) steers hidden states toward newer-fact representations at test time
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 时间吸引子引导（TAS）在测试时将隐藏状态引导至新事实表示
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 时间吸引子引导（TAS）在测试时将隐藏状态引导至新事实表示
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 时间吸引子引导（TAS）在测试时将隐藏状态引导至新事实表示
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Temporal Attractor Steering (TAS) for test-time hidden state steering to resolve parametric temporal conflicts without retraining.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文形式化了参数化时间冲突问题，并提出时间吸引子引导（TAS），一种三阶段测试时干预方法，检测冲突并识别关键层，通过激活修补将隐藏状态转向新事实表示。在四个开源模型上验证，单层激活修补即可高效解决时间事实冲突。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文形式化了参数化时间冲突问题，并提出时间吸引子引导（TAS），一种三阶段测试时干预方法，检测冲突并识别关键层，通过激活修补将隐藏状态转向新事实表示。在四个开源模型上验证，单层激活修补即可高效解决时间事实冲突。

quick_skim.title: Deeper is Not Always Better: Mitigating the Alignment Tax via Confident Layer Decoding
quick_skim.abstract: Autoregressive generation in large language models (LLMs) conventionally decodes from the final layer, assuming that deeper representations yield more reliable next-token predictions. We revisit this assumption by revealing a recurring Guess-Refine-Perturb dynamic: early layers form coarse guesses, intermediate layers refine reasoning-relevant semantics, and final layers can perturb these refined predictions toward generic or alignment-preferred tokens. We introduce Confident Decoding, a training-free decoding strategy that dynamically selects the most reliable near-final layer through entropy-guided conservative backward search. We further provide a theoretical formulation of layer selection as an optimal stopping problem, showing that under bounded projection noise and dominant late-stage alignment perturbation, our search rule filters perturbation while bounding the loss relative to the oracle refinement layer. Experiments across dense and Mixture-of-Experts LLMs demonstrate consistent gains on challenging reasoning benchmarks, including GPQA-Diamond, Omni-MATH, and HLE, with zero memory overhead and less than 2% latency increase. These results suggest dynamically bypassing final-layer perturbations can unlock stronger reasoning behavior from aligned LLMs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: mitigating alignment tax via decoding strategy
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过解码策略缓解对齐税
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过解码策略缓解对齐税
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过解码策略缓解对齐税
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Confident Decoding reduces alignment tax by selecting near-final layers via entropy-guided search, improving both safety and helpfulness without fine-tuning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文发现LLM解码时最终层可能引入对齐偏好导致性能下降（对齐税），提出Confident Decoding解码策略，通过熵引导的保守后向搜索动态选择最可靠的近最终层。实验表明该方法能在不进行微调的情况下有效缓解对齐税，同时保持安全性和有用性，为缓解微调后的安全退化提供了推理阶段的补充思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文发现LLM解码时最终层可能引入对齐偏好导致性能下降（对齐税），提出Confident Decoding解码策略，通过熵引导的保守后向搜索动态选择最可靠的近最终层。实验表明该方法能在不进行微调的情况下有效缓解对齐税，同时保持安全性和有用性，为缓解微调后的安全退化提供了推理阶段的补充思路。
